ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fried chicken food product class; no specific entry for deep fried chicken with Thai mango salad combination dish exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers fried chicken meat as the closest parent; the Thai mango salad component is not captured in this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific combination dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of deep fried chicken with Thai mango salad is approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 200 g of bone-in or boneless fried chicken pieces and 150 g of Thai green mango salad.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken (battered, deep fried) and green mango salad components estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (FDC ID 171477) and raw green mango nutritional profile (FDC ID 169910), combined in approximate 57:43 ratio by weight.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved source entry exists for this exact combination dish. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for batter-fried chicken (approximately 57% of serving weight) and Thai green mango salad components (approximately 43% of serving weight, including green mango, fish sauce, lime juice, chilli, shallots, dried shrimp, palm sugar). The combined per-100g values are derived from a weighted blend. IMR Malaysia MyFCD and HPB Singapore Foodo do not contain a direct entry for this dish. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used as the secondary global source per source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous foods: battered fried chicken (GI ~40–50, low GI due to protein and fat blunting glucose response) and green mango salad (GI ~41 for raw mango per University of Sydney GI Database). Composite GI estimated at 45.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep fried chicken with Thai mango salad. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on: (1) battered fried chicken has a low GI (~40–50) due to high protein and fat content slowing gastric emptying and glucose absorption; (2) raw green mango has a GI of approximately 41 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS et al., 2021, Am J Clin Nutr). Composite GI of 45 is a weighted estimate. GL per serving = (45 × 10.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 16.5 g. gi_category is low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1330 mg per serving) from fish sauce, dried shrimp, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium load from fish sauce and dried shrimp; if consumed, portion size should be restricted to half a standard serving and paired with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content (~10.5 g per 100 g) with low GI (~45) from batter and mango; palm sugar adds free sugars (~4.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index of approximately 45 means this dish produces a moderate glycemic response, but the glycemic load per serving (~16.5 g) is significant and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, limit the palm sugar dressing, and account for total carbohydrate intake within their meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep frying increases total fat (~10.8 g per 100 g) and saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g); cholesterol ~62 mg per 100 g; high sodium from condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish infrequently due to elevated total fat from deep frying, moderate saturated fat, and high sodium content; choosing a smaller portion and requesting reduced fish sauce dressing can help mitigate cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Deep Fried Chicken with Thai Mango Salad is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean Zi Char restaurant dish that combines crispy deep-fried chicken pieces with a vibrant Thai-style green mango salad. The chicken is typically marinated, coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter, and deep-fried until golden and crispy, yielding a juicy interior with a crunchy exterior. The accompanying Thai mango salad (yam mamuang) is made from shredded unripe green mango tossed with fish sauce, lime juice, palm sugar, fresh chilli, shallots, dried shrimp, and sometimes roasted peanuts, creating a balance of sour, sweet, salty, and spicy flavours characteristic of Thai cuisine. The dish reflects the multicultural culinary heritage of Malaysia and Singapore, where Thai flavour profiles are commonly incorporated into Zi Char (Chinese-style hawker cooking) menus. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate protein source (~13 g per 100 g) from the chicken, with elevated fat content (~10.8 g per 100 g) due to deep frying. Sodium is high (~380 mg per 100 g) primarily from fish sauce and dried shrimp, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low), as the high protein and fat content of the fried chicken blunts glucose absorption, though the glycemic load per standard serving (~16.5 g) is moderate. Clinically, this dish is best consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, fat, and free sugar content from the palm sugar dressing.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for this specific combination dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for batter-fried chicken and Thai green mango salad components in approximate serving proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this dish type in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy source used. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this estimated combination. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this estimated combination. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for estimation. The dish is served at Marina Seafood House - Boat Quay (field survey data) at SGD 22.00. The cuisine is classified as Malaysian Zi Char with Thai flavour influence. The food is likely halal as it is chicken-based with no pork ingredients, though restaurant-level halal certification should be verified independently. GI category is low per the threshold rule (GI 45 ≤ 55 = low). GL per serving calculated as (45 × 10.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 16.5 g.
